answers.question: List of scenarios
answers.evidence: The scenarios are: • Rapid Transition • Status Quo • Limited Climate Action
answers.location_hint: Strategy section, p.12; also p.10 Overview of Rakon's Climate Scenarios
answers.notes: Three scenarios used. Rapid Transition is based on IPCC SSP1-1.9 + IEA NZE 2050; Status Quo on IPCC SSP2-4.5 + IEA STEPS; Limited Climate Action on IPCC SSP5-8.5.

answers.question: Global temperature outcome by 2100
answers.answer: Three temperature outcomes by 2100 (relative to 1850–1900): 1.5°C (Rapid Transition), ~2.7°C (Status Quo), ~4.4°C (Limited Climate Action). The IEA Stated Policies scenario used to support Status Quo leads to a slightly lower ~2.4°C. No temperature overshoot is explicitly described.
answers.evidence: Rapid Transition 1.5°C … Status Quo 2.7°C … Limited climate action 4.4°C … 1 change in average global temperature by 2100 relative to 1850–1900 (°C)
answers.location_hint: p.10, Overview of Rakon's Climate Scenarios; also Appendix 1, p.27
answers.notes: The footnote explicitly states these are changes in average global temperature by 2100 relative to 1850–1900.

answers.question: Scenario provider
answers.answer: Multiple providers are explicitly named: IPCC (Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change) and IEA (International Energy Agency) as reference scenario providers; GeSI-CDP as the scenario analysis toolkit provider.
answers.evidence: We have used international reference scenarios to inform Rakon's scenario analysis. The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) is a body of the United Nations… The International Energy Agency (IEA) is an autonomous intergovernmental organisation… We have undertaken a global level scenario analysis exercise to support our assessment, using the GeSI-CDP Scenario Analysis Toolkit as the underlying tool for our scenario analysis process for three scenarios.
answers.notes: IPCC and IEA provide the reference scenarios; GeSI-CDP provides the toolkit framework used to build Rakon's own scenarios.

answers.question: Pick-and-mix of SSPs and RCPs
answers.evidence: The IPCC's sixth assessment reporting cycle provided SSP-RCP ('SSPX-Y') scenarios based on the Shared Socio-economic Pathways (SSPs), and partly informed by relevant Representative Concentration Pathways (RCPs) scenarios… Based on the following reference scenarios: IPCC SSP1-1.9 … IPCC SSP2-4.5 … IPCC SSP5-8.5
answers.notes: The document uses only SSP identifiers (SSP1-1.9, SSP2-4.5, SSP5-8.5) from the IPCC AR6 family. RCPs are mentioned only as background context explaining the SSP-RCP naming convention, not as standalone scenarios mixed with SSPs. No pick-and-mix of separate SSP and RCP families is present.

answers.question: Customised scenarios
answers.evidence: We have undertaken a global level scenario analysis exercise to support our assessment, using the GeSI-CDP Scenario Analysis Toolkit as the underlying tool for our scenario analysis process for three scenarios… External factors which are variable and influence the direction of change are referred to as driving forces of specific risks and opportunities in a particular scenario. These forces were identified as differing scenario parameters and assumptions for each scenario. … using an adapted risk assessment matrix with specific time horizons and the inclusion of three climate scenarios.
answers.notes: The scenarios are built using the GeSI-CDP toolkit applied to Rakon's specific business context, with custom scenario parameters and assumptions (Appendix 1). The risk assessment matrix is described as "adapted." Additionally, ClimSystems was engaged to produce location-specific physical risk reports for Rakon's sites. However, the word "customised" or "downscaled" is not used explicitly for the scenarios themselves; the adaptation is more in the application of the toolkit and the local factors analysis.

answers.question: Co-produced climate scenarios
answers.evidence: Scenario analysis is currently being conducted as a standalone exercise with input from several teams within the business. The Board has noted the scenario narratives and outputs. The scenario analysis project team reports to the General Counsel and Company Secretary. We intend to increase engagement with internal stakeholders in future as we continue to build Rakon's capability in this area.
answers.notes: The document describes internal team input but no participatory, co-created, deliberative, or stakeholder-collaborative scenario development with external parties. The document explicitly notes intent to increase internal stakeholder engagement in future, implying it has not yet occurred.

answers.question: Baseline scenarios
answers.answer: Yes — a baseline year of 2005 is referenced for the ClimSystems physical risk analysis (defined in the IPCC Sixth Assessment Report), but there is no separate "baseline scenario" against which the three climate scenarios are compared in the main scenario analysis.
answers.evidence: These climatic variables showed changes from the baseline (2005) defined in the IPCC Sixth Assessment Report for the following scenarios and time horizons: Scenarios SSP1-2.6 … SSP2-4.5 … SSP5-8.5 … Time horizons 2030 … 2050 … 2070
answers.notes: The 2005 baseline is used only for the ClimSystems physical risk reports (local factors analysis), not as a standalone scenario. There is no "business-as-usual" or "no-climate-change" baseline scenario described for the main scenario analysis framework.

answers.question: Model used
answers.evidence: We have undertaken a global level scenario analysis exercise to support our assessment, using the GeSI-CDP Scenario Analysis Toolkit as the underlying tool for our scenario analysis process for three scenarios… In FY24 we started that process by engaging ClimSystems to provide a climate-related physical risk report for Rakon India's manufacturing facility in India. In FY25 we extended that exercise to consider Rakon's operations in New Zealand, the UK, and at two Rakon France sites in France.
answers.notes: No IAMs (e.g. REMIND, MESSAGE, GCAM) or macroeconomic models are named. The GeSI-CDP toolkit and ClimSystems are the only named analytical tools/models. IPCC and IEA are reference scenario sources, not models per se.

answers.question: Damage from acute physical risk considered
answers.evidence: The output from the ClimSystems reports identified some physical risks for Rakon's locations (both acute and chronic) associated with climate driven events. The only climatic variable that features as a higher risk within Rakon's long-term time horizon is: • Extreme precipitation (2 locations) – this is daily extreme rainfall leading to hazards such as flooding that impacts Rakon's buildings and operations.
answers.notes: Acute physical risks (extreme precipitation/flooding) are explicitly identified and assessed for specific locations. However, quantified financial damage/loss estimates are not provided (the document notes anticipated financial impacts have been deferred under an adoption provision).

answers.question: Non-temperature variables in scenarios
answer: energy mix (low emissions electricity generation share)
technology investment (solar PV and wind capacity)
answers.evidence: Key Scenario Metrics: Carbon Price … Energy demand … Energy mix … Forestry and Afforestation … Technology investment … The only climatic variable that features as a higher risk within Rakon's long-term time horizon is: • Extreme precipitation (2 locations) … Monthly precipitation (1 location) – changes in the patterns of rainfall … Heatwave days (1 location) … rising sea levels beyond [the medium term]
answers.notes: Variables drawn from both the scenario parameters tables (Appendix 1) and the ClimSystems physical risk analysis (p.14) and scenario narratives (p.30).

answers.question: Scale of scenarios
answers.evidence: We have undertaken a global level scenario analysis exercise to support our assessment… Part of achieving greater maturity in our approach to managing climate-related risks is to include additional consideration of local factors. In FY24 we started that process by engaging ClimSystems to provide a climate-related physical risk report for Rakon India's manufacturing facility in India. In FY25 we extended that exercise to consider Rakon's operations in New Zealand, the UK, and at two Rakon France sites in France, bringing the total number of assessed locations to five.
answers.notes: The main scenario analysis is global; the ClimSystems physical risk reports provide site/location-level (regional/local) analysis for five specific Rakon facilities.

answers.question: Time frames
answers.answer: Short-term: 1–3 years (approx. 2027); Medium-term: 4–10 years (approx. 2030); Long-term: >25 years (approx. 2050+). Rationales are explicitly provided for each timeframe.
answers.evidence: Short term: Time horizon 1-3 years, Approx. year (rel. to 2025)* 2027, Rationale: Aligns with Rakon's 3-year business planning horizon for strategy purposes. Medium term: Time horizon 4-10 years, Approx. year (rel. to 2025)* 2030, Rationale: Aligns with interim international emissions reduction targets / Aligns with Rakon's current capital expenditure time horizon of up to five years. Long term: Time horizon >25 years, Approx. year (rel. to 2025)* 2050+, Rationale: Aligns with international emissions reduction targets.
answers.location_hint: p.11 (scenario analysis time horizons table); also p.18 (risk management time horizons)
answers.notes: Rationales are explicitly stated for all three timeframes. The IPCC reference scenarios extend to 2100, and ClimSystems analysis also covers 2070 (beyond Rakon's long-term horizon).

answers.question: Justification for scenario/model choice
answers.evidence: Key reasons for selecting these reference climate scenarios for use by Rakon were: • reference sources for the scenarios are internationally recognised and widely used for this purpose; • as a Climate Reporting Entity with a global footprint, it is appropriate for Rakon to use scenarios that have international relevance and coverage; • the selected scenarios and associated temperature increases meet the XRB's requirements for scenarios as set out in NZ CS1, i.e., at a minimum, a 1.5 degrees Celsius climate-related scenario, a 3 degrees Celsius or greater climate-related scenario, and a third climate-related scenario; and • the selected scenarios have time horizons that cover the time horizons chosen by Rakon. Key reasons for selecting the toolkit were: • CDP is a well-established and respected provider in the sustainability arena and has partnered with GeSI to deliver this toolkit for the specific purpose of enabling entities like Rakon to develop scenario analysis; and • Purchase of the scenario analysis toolkit enabled Rakon to access climate risks and opportunities disclosed by Rakon's global peers at sector level.
answers.location_hint: p.9 (toolkit justification) and p.11 (reference scenario justification)
answers.notes: Explicit justifications are provided for both the choice of the GeSI-CDP toolkit and the choice of IPCC/IEA reference scenarios.
